drafts.text:
Today Jot hits Hacker News for the first time.

I built a free, open-source Mac dictation app: press a hotkey, speak, text lands wherever your cursor is — all on the Neural Engine, no server, no account, no audio leaves your Mac.

128 downloads. Zero paid promotion. 61 days since v1.0.

If you've launched something on HN — how was day one?

#indiedev #macOS #buildinpublic
drafts.note: Attach bold-claim.svg (export as 1080×1080 PNG). Do NOT put the link in the post body — LinkedIn downranks posts with external links. Paste the utm_url as the FIRST COMMENT immediately after posting. The closing question invites builders to share HN launch stories — high engagement trigger.

drafts.text: Show HN is live: Jot — free, on-device Mac dictation. Hotkey → speak → text at cursor. 128 downloads. Zero ads. 61 days. Built it because I was done paying $85/yr for this.
drafts.note: Attach bold-claim.svg (export as PNG). No link in the post — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. Consider a second X post attaching slide-1.svg as a carousel teaser later in the window.

drafts.text:
I started dictating instead of typing 3 months ago — what actually changed

Three months ago I switched to dictating most of my writing — not transcription of recorded audio, but live dictation: pressing a hotkey and speaking directly into whatever app I'm in.

What I expected: marginally faster first draft. What I got: genuinely different writing habits.

**You write more** because typing is no longer the bottleneck. When speaking, you capture thoughts before you self-censor them. I estimate I write 2× more now, first-draft.

**The friction has to be zero.** I tried tools that needed a window switch or a record button. Never stuck. The one I landed on (Jot — disclosure, I built it) pastes text directly at the cursor in whatever app. No step zero.

**On-device matters for speed, not just privacy.** Local processing returns text in under a second. Cloud round-trips take 2–4s. That latency gap kills flow exactly when you want it least.

**Things I dictate now that I'd never have typed:** commit messages, code comments, PR descriptions, Slack replies, notes during calls.

Has anyone else made this switch? What broke for you?
drafts.note: Post to r/productivity (rotation: macapps Jun 11, MacOS Jun 12, productivity Jun 13, macapps Jun 14, MacOS Jun 15, productivity Jun 16). First line is the post title; rest is the body. drafts.text: Show HN: Jot – free, on-device Mac dictation, picks the right model for your language
drafts.publish_window_pt: 6:00–8:00am ET TODAY (Tue Jun 16) — post immediately, do not delay
drafts.note:
Post TODAY — prepared in the June 14 brief, held until Tuesday. Submission URL: use the utm_url above. First comment (post immediately after submitting):

'Hey HN. I'm Vineet, I built Jot.

The short version: press a hotkey, speak, and the transcript pastes wherever your cursor is in any app. Transcription runs on the Apple Neural Engine using NVIDIA's Parakeet model — no internet, no account, no audio leaves the Mac.

What pushed me to build it: I was paying $85/year for a cloud dictation app and realized the gap between what you pay and what it costs to run locally had collapsed.

Technical details:
- Audio capture: AVAudioEngine → AVAudioConverter → 16kHz mono Float32
- Transcription: FluidAudio wrapping Parakeet TDT 0.6B v3
- Delivery: clipboard sandwich — save → write → synthetic ⌘V → restore
- Status UI: NSPanel floating pill under the notch (Dynamic Island-style)
- Hotkeys: KeyboardShortcuts library; Esc only captured during active recording

v1.14.1 added language-based model selection. Instead of choosing Parakeet v2/v3/Nemotron, pick the language you speak — Jot picks the best model.

There is also a rewrite-with-voice mode: select text, press a hotkey, speak an instruction (make this shorter, translate to Spanish), Jot rewrites in place.

128 downloads so far, zero paid promotion. Apple Silicon only, macOS 14+.

Source: https://github.com/vineetu/JOT-Transcribe'

NEVER solicit upvotes. Stay in the thread all morning — the HN audience will ask about NSPanel, the clipboard sandwich, FluidAudio, and the rewrite pipeline. These are the best questions to answer.

notes: Show HN day — the June 14 brief prepared a Show HN post for today (Tue Jun 16, 6–8am ET). Total downloads 128 (+2 from 126; avg7 = 6/day). v1.14.1 went from 7 to 9 downloads; all other releases stable. iTunes API returned HTTP 403 for the third consecutive day; appstore.json preserved at June 13 values (iOS v1.1, 1 rating at 5★). No specific Reddit/HN threads found within 7 days for opportunistic replies — active competitors (Onit, Spokenly, VoxTap, Voibe) being discussed but without verifiable recent thread URLs. Jot absent from every Mac dictation roundup found (getvoibe.com, embertype.com, machow2.com, setapp.com, onresonant.com). AlternativeTo.net Superwhisper/Wispr Flow alternatives pages are the easiest zero-cost listing — submit Jot there this week. Outreach window opens June 19 (7 days after June 12 last outreach). The reddit draft held today could go live June 19 with recommended:true on r/productivity — the 'builder who switched from paying to building' angle fits that community well.
